From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Chiang Mai Province](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Mai_Province&redirect=no "Chiang Mai Province"))

Largest province of Thailand

Not to be confused with [Chiang Mai](/wiki/Chiang_Mai "Chiang Mai"), [Mueang Chiang Mai district](/wiki/Mueang_Chiang_Mai_district "Mueang Chiang Mai district"), or [Chiang Rai province](/wiki/Chiang_Rai_province "Chiang Rai province").

This article is about the first-level administrative division in Thailand. For other uses, see [Chiang Mai (disambiguation)](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_(disambiguation) "Chiang Mai (disambiguation)").

**Chiang Mai**[[a]](#cite_note-6) is the largest [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_Thailand "Provinces of Thailand") (*changwat*) of [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand") by area.[[6]](#cite_note-area1-7)[[7]](#cite_note-area2-8) It lies in [upper northern Thailand](/wiki/Northern_Thailand#Regional_classification_of_northern_Thailand "Northern Thailand") and has a population of 1.78 million people. It is bordered by [Chiang Rai](/wiki/Chiang_Rai_province "Chiang Rai province") to the northeast, [Lampang](/wiki/Lampang_province "Lampang province") and [Lamphun](/wiki/Lamphun_province "Lamphun province") to the south, [Tak](/wiki/Tak_province "Tak province") to the southwest, [Mae Hong Son](/wiki/Mae_Hong_Son_province "Mae Hong Son province") to the west, and [Shan State](/wiki/Shan_State "Shan State") of [Burma](/wiki/Burma "Burma") to the north. The capital, [Chiang Mai](/wiki/Chiang_Mai "Chiang Mai"), is 685 kilometres (426 mi) north of Bangkok.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Mai_province&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

Chiang Mai province is about 685 km (426 mi) from Bangkok in the [Mae Ping](/wiki/Ping_River "Ping River") River basin and is on average at 300 m (1,000 ft) elevation. Surrounded by the mountain ranges of the [Thai highlands](/wiki/Thai_highlands "Thai highlands"), it covers an area of approximately 22,311 km2 (9,000 sq mi). The mountains of the [Daen Lao Range](/wiki/Daen_Lao_Range "Daen Lao Range") (ทิวเขาแดนลาว) at the north end of the province, the [Thanon Thong Chai Range](/wiki/Thanon_Thong_Chai_Range "Thanon Thong Chai Range") (เทือกเขาถนนธงชัย) with the highest mountain in Thailand, [Doi Inthanon](/wiki/Doi_Inthanon "Doi Inthanon") at 2,565 metres (8,415 ft), stretching in a north–south direction, and the [Khun Tan Range](/wiki/Khun_Tan_Range "Khun Tan Range") in the east of the province are covered by [rain forest](/wiki/Rain_forest "Rain forest"). The Mae Ping, one of the major tributaries of the [Chao Phraya River](/wiki/Chao_Phraya_River "Chao Phraya River"), originates in the [Daen Lao](/wiki/Daen_Lao_Range "Daen Lao Range") mountains. Several [national parks](/wiki/National_parks_(Thailand) "National parks (Thailand)") are in the province: [Doi Inthanon](/wiki/Doi_Inthanon_National_Park "Doi Inthanon National Park"),[[8]](#cite_note-9) [Doi Suthep-Pui](/wiki/Doi_Suthep-Pui_National_Park "Doi Suthep-Pui National Park"),[[9]](#cite_note-10) [Ob Luang](/wiki/Ob_Luang_National_Park "Ob Luang National Park"),[[10]](#cite_note-11) [Sri Lanna](/wiki/Sri_Lanna_National_Park "Sri Lanna National Park"),[[11]](#cite_note-12) [Huai Nam Dang](/wiki/Huai_Nam_Dang_National_Park "Huai Nam Dang National Park"),[[12]](#cite_note-13) [Mae Wang](/wiki/Mae_Wang_National_Park "Mae Wang National Park"),[[13]](#cite_note-14) and [Pha Daeng](/wiki/Pha_Daeng_National_Park "Pha Daeng National Park").[[14]](#cite_note-15) The total forest area is 15,404 km2 (5,948 sq mi) or 69.6 percent of provincial area.[[15]](#cite_note-16)

## Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Mai_province&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Climate")]

Chiang Mai has a [tropical wet and dry climate](/wiki/Tropical_wet_and_dry_climate "Tropical wet and dry climate") ([Köppen](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification") *Aw*), tempered by the low latitude and moderate elevation, with warm to hot weather year-round, though nighttime conditions during the dry season can be cool and are much lower than daytime highs. The maximum temperature ever recorded is 42.4 °C (108.3 °F) in May 2005.[[19]](#cite_note-MaxTemperature-20)

Of the population, 13.4 percent in the province are members of [hill tribes](/wiki/Hill_tribe_(Thailand) "Hill tribe (Thailand)")

* **[Northern Thai people](/wiki/Northern_Thai_people "Northern Thai people")** or **[Tai Yuan](/wiki/Northern_Thai_people "Northern Thai people")** native to nine provinces in [Northern Thailand](/wiki/Northern_Thailand "Northern Thailand"), principally in the area of the former kingdom of [Lan Na](/wiki/Lan_Na "Lan Na").
* **[Tai Lue](/wiki/Dai_people "Dai people")** live in dwellings of usually only a single room wooden house built on high poles. They are skilled in weaving.
* **[Tai Yai](/wiki/Shan_people "Shan people")**, Burmese in origin, harvest rice, farm, raise cattle and trade. Their craftsmanship lies in weaving, pottery, wood carving and bronze ware.
* **[Akha](/wiki/Akha_people "Akha people")** the largest population of any hill tribe in the region. Originating from Tibet and Southern China, they dwell on high ground around 1,200 metres (3,937 ft) above sea-level. Within their villages they build a spirit gateway to protect them from evil spirits.
* **[Hmong](/wiki/Hmong_people "Hmong people")** from southern China. Prefer higher elevations. They raise livestock and grow rice, corn, tobacco, and cabbage. Known for their embroidery and silver.
* **[Karen](/wiki/Karen_people "Karen people")** occupy valleys and riverbanks.
* **[Lahu](/wiki/Lahu_people "Lahu people")** from southern China and live in high areas. They are known as hunters and planters.
* **[Lisu](/wiki/Lisu_people "Lisu people")** from southern China and Tibet are renowned for their colorful dress and also build their dwellings on high poles. They harvest rice and corn and their men are skilled in hunting.
* **[Yao](/wiki/Yao_people_(Asia) "Yao people (Asia)")** reside on mountainsides and grow corn and other crops. They are skilled blacksmiths, silversmiths and embroiders.
* **[Mon](/wiki/Mon_people "Mon people")** native to [Haripuñjaya](/wiki/Haripu%C3%B1jaya "Haripuñjaya") Where the capital was at [Lamphun](/wiki/Lamphun "Lamphun")

## Symbols

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Mai_province&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Symbols")]

The [seal of the province](/wiki/Seals_of_the_provinces_of_Thailand "Seals of the provinces of Thailand") shows a [white elephant](/wiki/White_elephant "White elephant") in a glass pavilion. The white elephant is a royal symbol in Thailand, and it is depicted to remember the offering of a white elephant by [Thammalangka](/wiki/Thammalangka "Thammalangka"), a ruler of Chiang Mai, to his overlord, King [Rama II](/wiki/Rama_II_of_Siam "Rama II of Siam") of Bangkok. The pavilion symbolizes that Buddhism prospered in Chiang Mai, especially when in 1477 the teachings of Buddha, the [Tripitaka](/wiki/Tripitaka "Tripitaka"), were reviewed.[[20]](#cite_note-RoySoc-21)

The provincial flower and [tree](/wiki/List_of_Thai_provincial_trees "List of Thai provincial trees") is the "flame of the forest" (*[Butea monosperma](/wiki/Butea_monosperma "Butea monosperma")*).[[20]](#cite_note-RoySoc-21) The edible [cyprinid](/wiki/Cyprinid "Cyprinid") fish "[black sharkminnow](/wiki/Black_sharkminnow "Black sharkminnow")" (*[Labeo chrysophekadion](/wiki/Labeo_chrysophekadion "Labeo chrysophekadion")*) is the provincial aquatic life.

The provincial slogan is *In the shadow of Mount Doi Suthep, blessed with rice customs and traditions, beautiful wild flowers, magnificent Nakhon Phing*.[[20]](#cite_note-RoySoc-21)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Mai_province&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: History")]

Main articles: [Kingdom of Chiang Mai](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Chiang_Mai "Kingdom of Chiang Mai") and [Lan Na Kingdom](/wiki/Lan_Na_Kingdom "Lan Na Kingdom")

The city of [Chiang Mai](/wiki/Chiang_Mai "Chiang Mai"), the capital of Chiang Mai province, was also capital of the [Lanna](/wiki/Lanna "Lanna") Kingdom after its founding in 1296, during the same period of time as the establishment of the [Sukhothai Kingdom](/wiki/Sukhothai_Kingdom "Sukhothai Kingdom").[[21]](#cite_note-TAT-22) From then, Chiang Mai not only became the capital and cultural core of the Lanna Kingdom, but also the centre of Buddhism in northern Thailand. King [Meng Rai](/wiki/Mangrai_the_Great "Mangrai the Great") built many temples in the region.

In 1558, Chiang Mai became a colony of the [First Toungoo Empire](/wiki/First_Toungoo_Empire "First Toungoo Empire"). Chiang Mai remained its colony for more than 200 years, until the [Burmese–Siamese War (1775–1776)](/wiki/Burmese%E2%80%93Siamese_War_(1775%E2%80%931776) "Burmese–Siamese War (1775–1776)"). In 1774 the Burmese colonial regime were finally driven out of Chiang Mai by a coalition of Lanna and Siamese forces and it then became a tributary state of [Siam](/wiki/Siam "Siam"), which later installed a [Lanna](/wiki/Lanna "Lanna") chieftain ally, [Kawila](/wiki/Kawila "Kawila"), to independently rule over Lampang and Chiang Mai region as a monarch.

In the reign of [King Rama V](/wiki/King_Rama_V "King Rama V") of Siam, under his administrative centralization policy and due to the ineptitude of Chiang Mai's ruling family, Chiang Mai eventually lost its independence, was annexed and became a second level subdivision of Siam.[[22]](#cite_note-23)

From 1933 on, Chiang Mai received its status as a "province" of Siam and has remained so until the present day.[[23]](#cite_note-24)

## Administrative divisions and postal codes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Mai_province&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Administrative divisions and postal codes")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3a/Districts_Chiang_Mai.svg/330px-Districts_Chiang_Mai.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Districts_Chiang_Mai.svg)

Map of twenty five districts

### Local government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Mai_province&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Local government")]

As of 26 November 2019 there are:[[25]](#cite_note-26) one Chiang Mai Provincial Administration Organisation (**ongkan borihan suan changwat**) and 121 municipal (*thesaban*) areas in the province. Chiang Mai has city (*[thesaban nakhon](/wiki/Thesaban_nakhon "Thesaban nakhon")*) status. Mae Jo, Mae Hia, Mueang Kaen Phatthana and Ton Pao have town (*[thesaban mueang](/wiki/Thesaban_mueang "Thesaban mueang")*) status. Further 116 subdistrict municipalities (*[thesaban tambon](/wiki/Thesaban_tambon "Thesaban tambon")*). The non-municipal areas are administered by 89 Subdistrict Administrative Organisations - SAO (*ongkan borihan suan tambon*).

## Transportation

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/48/RTN_Embraer_ERJ_135LR_beneath_Wat_Doi_Suthep.jpg/250px-RTN_Embraer_ERJ_135LR_beneath_Wat_Doi_Suthep.jpg)](/wiki/File:RTN_Embraer_ERJ_135LR_beneath_Wat_Doi_Suthep.jpg)

[Chiang Mai International Airport](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_International_Airport "Chiang Mai International Airport") showing [Doi Suthep temple](/wiki/Wat_Phra_That_Doi_Suthep "Wat Phra That Doi Suthep") in the upper left corner

* [Road](/wiki/Thai_highway_network#Northern_Thailand "Thai highway network"): Chiang Mai is on [Highway 11](/wiki/Thailand_Route_11 "Thailand Route 11"), the only 2-digit road number in the province. Other roads connect the city of Chiang Mai with the north and south of the province, as well as with the east towards Chiang Rai province.
* Train: [Chiang Mai railway station](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_railway_station "Chiang Mai railway station") is the northern terminus of the [Northern Line](/wiki/Northern_Line_(Thailand) "Northern Line (Thailand)"), operated by the [State Railway of Thailand](/wiki/State_Railway_of_Thailand "State Railway of Thailand").
* Air: [Chiang Mai International Airport](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_International_Airport "Chiang Mai International Airport") (CNX) is one of the seven Thai international airports under the aegis of the [Airports of Thailand Public Company Limited](/wiki/Airports_of_Thailand_PLC "Airports of Thailand PLC") (AOT).[[26]](#cite_note-27) As Chiang Mai International Airport is the major gateway to northern Thailand, it plays an important role in promoting travel and tourism throughout the northern region. Today, 14 airlines serve the airport and more than 3,000,000 passengers, 15,000 flights, and 16,000 tons of cargo are handled annually.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Tourism

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Mai_province&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Tourism")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This article **is written like a [travel guide](/wiki/Wikipedia:What_Wikipedia_is_not#Wikipedia_is_not_a_manual,_guidebook,_textbook,_or_scientific_journal "Wikipedia:What Wikipedia is not")**. Please help [rewrite it](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Mai_province&action=edit) in a [neutral](/wiki/Wikipedia:NPOV "Wikipedia:NPOV"), [encyclopedic](/wiki/Wikipedia:Encyclopedic_style "Wikipedia:Encyclopedic style") style or move its content to [Wikivoyage](https://en.wikivoyage.org/wiki/ "voy:"). *(February 2020)* |

Chiang Mai province is the tourist hub of the north and one of Thailand's most important tourist destinations.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] It is considered one of the most scenic provinces in the country due to its mountain ranges, valleys, flora, and fauna. For example, the [Hang Dong Canyon](/wiki/Hang_Dong_Canyon "Hang Dong Canyon") is coveted as a great tourist attraction. Furthermore, unlike most of Thailand, in some months, the climate in the north and Chiang Mai is cool, fresh, and misty.

## Health

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Mai_province&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Health")]

Each amphoe of Chiang Mai has its own hospital, but among the largest are located in Mueang Chiang Mai District and include [Maharaj Nakorn Chiang Mai Hospital](/wiki/Maharaj_Nakorn_Chiang_Mai_Hospital "Maharaj Nakorn Chiang Mai Hospital") and [Nakornping Hospital](/wiki/Nakornping_Hospital "Nakornping Hospital").

## Local products

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Mai_province&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Local products")]

Chiang Mai is a handicrafts centre, with a variety of antiques, silver jewellery, and embroidery, Thai silks and cottons, basketry, celadon, silverware, furniture, lacquerware, woodcarvings, and parasols.[[27]](#cite_note-28)

## Local culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Mai_province&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Local culture")]

The north of Thailand's culture is Lanna in origin and the people are proud of their northern roots. The region is home to distinctive foods, music, arts, way of life, and even language. Chiang Mai is home to various [hill tribes](/wiki/Hill_tribe_(Thailand) "Hill tribe (Thailand)") and their own distinctive cultures.

### Local food

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Mai_province&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Local food")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c5/Drumstick_khao_soy.JPG/250px-Drumstick_khao_soy.JPG)](/wiki/File:Drumstick_khao_soy.JPG)

*Khao soi*

See also: [Thai cuisine](/wiki/Thai_cuisine "Thai cuisine")

* **[Nam phrik ong](/wiki/Nam_phrik_ong "Nam phrik ong")** is a type of *[Nam phrik](/wiki/Nam_phrik "Nam phrik")* chili paste which is made of minced pork and tomatoes. It is usually eaten with soft-boiled vegetables, pork crackling, or deep-fried crunchy rice cakes.
* **[Nam phrik num](/wiki/Nam_phrik_num "Nam phrik num")** meaning "chili paste young man", is another kind of paste which can be eaten with pork crackling.
* **[Sai ua](/wiki/Sai_ua "Sai ua")** is a local sausage that is very aromatic and spicy and is usually eaten with sticky rice.
* **Kaeng** meaning "curry", is not made with coconut milk in the north.
  + [Kaeng hang-le](/w/index.php?title=Kaeng_hangle&action=edit&redlink=1 "Kaeng hangle (page does not exist)") is northern-style pork curry
  + Kaeng om is a spicy curry consisting of intestines
  + [Kaeng khae](/wiki/Kaeng_khae "Kaeng khae") is a spicy curry consisting mainly of vegetables.
* **[Khanom chin](/wiki/Khanom_chin "Khanom chin") [nam ngiao](/wiki/Nam_ngiao "Nam ngiao")** is a traditional northern noodle dish with chicken or pork.
* **[Khao soi](/wiki/Khao_soi "Khao soi")** is a noodle dish which can be made from chicken, pork, or beef made with coconut milk and garnished with chopped fresh shallots, pickled cabbage, chilli paste to taste, and a slice of fresh lime.

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Mai_province&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Sports")]

There are two main sport stadia in Chiang Mai and its environs: [700th Anniversary Stadium](/wiki/700th_Anniversary_Stadium "700th Anniversary Stadium") and Province Stadium. 700th Anniversary Stadium is on Klongchonpratan Road, 7 km (4.3 mi) from Chiang Mai University. There are swimming pools, diving pool, basketball arena, and 11 tennis courts.
